name: Racquets and rivalries
description: From drawing on player likenesses to fictionalising the drama of historic matches, explore how tennis has inspired screen makers to bring the game to life on screen.

key_work.title: Collector's cabinet: Australian tennis games on the world stage
key_work.brief_description: Tennis is one of the oldest videogame genres – one of the world’s first games was Tennis for Two (1958). From the earliest days of play, the sport has tested the limits of technology and design, whether through Pong (1972) or Wii Sports (2006).

constellations_primary.name: Racquets and rivalries
constellations_primary.description: From drawing on player likenesses to fictionalising the drama of historic matches, explore how tennis has inspired screen makers to bring the game to life on screen.

end.title: Naomi Osaka
end.creator_credit: David Brodie, Katy Murakami, Lauren Cioffi, Sally Rosen, Film 45 and UNINTERRUPTED
end.brief_description: An intimate look inside the life of one of the most gifted and complex athletes of her generation offers insight into the tough decisions and ecstatic triumphs that shape Naomi Osaka as both an elite global superstar and a young woman navigating a pressure-filled world.

constellations_other.name: Racquets and rivalries
constellations_other.description: From drawing on player likenesses to fictionalising the drama of historic matches, explore how tennis has inspired screen makers to bring the game to life on screen.

links.description: The documentary series follows Naomi Osaka after her first grand slam win in 2018. It was in this same year that AO International Tennis was released, where Osaka was featured as a playable character.

end.title: Challengers
end.creator_credit: Luca Guadagnino, Jonathan Anderson, Margaret Robbs, Marco Costa, Amy Pascal, Rachel O'Connor, Zendaya, Amazon MGM Studios, Frenesy Film, Pascal Pictures, Why Are You Acting? Productions, Merissa Lombardo, Justin Kuritzkes, Chris Payne, Erik Mnatsakanyan, Fabio Cerrito, James P. Noon and Sean Martin
end.headline_credit: Italy and United States of America, 2024
end.brief_description: Tennis player turned coach Tashi has taken her husband, Art, and transformed him into a world-famous Major champion. To jolt him out of his recent losing streak, she signs him up for a “Challenger” event — close to the lowest level of pro tournament — where he finds himself standing across the net from his former best friend and Tashi’s former boyfriend.

constellations_other.name: Racquets and rivalries
constellations_other.description: From drawing on player likenesses to fictionalising the drama of historic matches, explore how tennis has inspired screen makers to bring the game to life on screen.

links.description: Photogrammetry was used to make professional tennis players playable characters in AO International Tennis. Making the film Challengers similarly used photogrammetry to capture the likeness of actors for facial replacements over their tennis doubles in rally sequences.

links.description: Footage from the 2018 U.S. Open singles final between Serena Williams and Naomi Osaka serves as the starting point for the Netflix docu-series Naomi Osaka. This match similarly prompted writer Justin Kuritzkes to write the Challengers film script.

video_links.name: BORG VS. MCENROE [Trailer] – In theaters & On Demand 4/13

end.brief_description: The Swedish Björn Borg and the American John McEnroe, the best tennis players in the world, maintain a legendary duel during the 1980 Wimbledon tournament.

constellations_other.name: Racquets and rivalries
constellations_other.description: From drawing on player likenesses to fictionalising the drama of historic matches, explore how tennis has inspired screen makers to bring the game to life on screen.

links.description: Rivalries and interpersonal relationships are at the centre of both films. While Challengers focuses on how personal rivalries and relationships play out in professional tennis, Borg vs McEnroe fictionalises the professional rivalry and later friendship between two players.

start.title: Borg vs McEnroe

end.title: The Royal Tenenbaums [DVD]
end.creator_credit: Wes Anderson, Barry Mendel, American Empirical Pictures and Touchstone Pictures
end.brief_description: Three children - all precocious geniuses in their youth - struggle with the trials of adulthood in this eccentric black comedy from Wes Anderson, writer and director of “Bottle rocket” and “Rushmore”. In their pre-teens these three suffered the departure of their corrupt father, Royal, disintegrating familial harmony. Twenty-two years later a remorseful and estranged Royal seeks to return to the fold, on the pretext that he has terminal cancer. Now adults, the siblings feel like abject failures at life and are beset by excessive emotional problems, principally emanating from Royal’s off-hand mistreatment of them. The characters that occupy this fantastical world are imbued with an aching melancholia not dissimilar in tone to the writings of J.D. Salinger. Indeed the Tenenbaum children can’t help but resemble the precociously talented yet tragically dysfunctional Glass siblings of Salinger’s iconic text, each living with thwarted and frustrated desires. Utilising a series of vignettes and by presenting surreal, exaggerated interiors, saturated with character history and nuance, Anderson continues his exploration of the themes of the outsider, family, and the difficult transition to adulthood. This enchanting film is a finely detailed and mannered affair that whilst not eliciting uproarious laughs, successfully amuses in a wry, offbeat and endearingly eccentric manner. In an era when the Farrelly brothers and Adam Sandler dominate the comedy genre, Anderson represents a left-of-field auteur with a delightfully skewed perspective on notions of narrative, entertainment and cinema. Stellar cast includes: Gene Hackman, Anjelica Huston, Ben Stiller, Gwyneth Paltrow, Luke Wilson, Owen Wilson, Danny Glover and Bill Murray.

constellations_other.name: Racquets and rivalries
constellations_other.description: From drawing on player likenesses to fictionalising the drama of historic matches, explore how tennis has inspired screen makers to bring the game to life on screen.

links.description: The character Richie Tenenbaum from The Royal Tenenbaums is loosely based on pro-tennis player Björn Borg. Björn Borg is later directly portrayed on screen in the biopic Borg vs McEnroe.

end.title: Battle of the Sexes
end.creator_credit: Jonathan Dayton, Valerie Faris, Mary Zophres, Pamela Martin, Christian Colson, Danny Boyle, Robert Graf, Cloud Eight Films, Decibel Films, Fox Searchlight Pictures, Ingenious Media, Judy Becker, David Robinson, Edson Williams and Simon Beaufoy
end.headline_credit: United Kingdom and United States of America, 2017
end.brief_description: The true story of the 1973 tennis match between world number one Billie Jean King and ex-champ and serial hustler Bobby Riggs.

constellations_other.name: Racquets and rivalries
constellations_other.description: From drawing on player likenesses to fictionalising the drama of historic matches, explore how tennis has inspired screen makers to bring the game to life on screen.

links.description: Borg vs McEnroe was centred on the 1980 Wimbledon Men’s singles final between Björn Borg and John McEnroe. Battle of the Sexes was similarly centred on a historic tennis match, the 1973 exhibition match Battle of the Sexes.
